text: I arrive as career switcher deciding whether this can replace another course. My past: I have bought online courses before and often quit when the path became vague after the first week.
Today I want to: Understand what I would learn, how long it takes, and whether there is proof it works.
My life goal is: Build a career that gives me independence without wasting money on empty promises.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

text: I see "self-degree - AI Learning Platform That Replaces Traditional School". 🇬🇧 AI EDUCATIONAL PLATFORM Stop Spending 10+ Hours Planning Curriculum. I think: This looks aimed at homeschool parents and children, not someone like me trying to switch careers.
My reaction: The speed claims sound attractive, but they feel too broad without proof, timelines, or an adult career path.
Idea: Show separate paths for career switchers, with sample outcomes, time estimates, and evidence from real learners.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month(s) of life to keep digging is not worth it unless the product clearly helps me build career independence without empty promises.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that this does not currently fit my goal; I am fairly confident because the page is framed around replacing school for children, not replacing an adult course.
